Title: Nitrate restricts nodule organogenesis through inhibition of cytokinin biosynthesis in Lotus japonicus

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes nodule organogenesis and supports nodule maturation and nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in the root susceptible zone at one and/or two days after rhizobia inoculation; at 2 dpi its induction is reduced by high nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in the root susceptible zone after rhizobia inoculation; nitrate suppresses induction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizobial inoculation with Mesorhizobium loti; high nitrate (5 mM KNO3); cytokinin rescue with 6-benzylaminopurine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Ipt3 increases sensitivity to nitrate inhibition of nodule maturation and nitrogen fixation and contributes to reduced root iP content
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not established; contributes redundantly to cytokinin biosynthesis under nodulation conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LORE1 loss-of-function mutants; cytokinin quantification; qRT-PCR; nodulation scoring; acetylene reduction assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants show stronger nitrate-sensitive loss of mature nodules and nitrogen fixation; single mutant phenotype is modest under nitrate-free conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA642098; cytokinin metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS3492
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previous reports gave variable nodulation phenotypes for Ljipt3; in this study iP content rose after inoculation likely due to redundant activity including LjIpt2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjIpt3 contributes to symbiosis-induced cytokinin biosynthesis in the root susceptible zone. 2. High nitrate attenuates LjIpt3-associated cytokinin accumulation and ipt3 mutants become hypersensitive to nitrate inhibition of nodule maturation and nitrogen fixation. 3. LjIpt3 acts redundantly with LjIpt4 in maintaining adequate iP for nodulation under nitrate stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing LjIpt3 activity could improve nodulation robustness under moderate nitrate, but cytokinin dosage must be finely tuned because excess cytokinin can inhibit nodulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes nodule organogenesis and supports nodule maturation and nitrogen fixation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after rhizobial inoculation; elevated in uninoculated nitrate conditions but reduced at 1 dpi in nitrate relative to nitrate-free inoculated roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in root susceptible zone during nodulation; responsive to rhizobia and nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizobial inoculation with Mesorhizobium loti; high nitrate (5 mM KNO3); cytokinin rescue with 6-benzylaminopurine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ipt4 is required for resistance to nitrate inhibition of nodule initiation, maturation, and nitrogen fixation; mutants show reduced iP under high nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not established; important redundant cytokinin biosynthetic enzyme for nitrate resilience of nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LORE1 loss-of-function mutants; double-mutant analysis; cytokinin quantification; qRT-PCR; nodulation scoring; nodule primordia scoring; acetylene reduction assay; genetic interaction with nlp mutants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ipt4 mutants show stronger loss of nodulation and nitrogen fixation in nitrate; cytokinin supplementation can rescue but excess cytokinin inhibits root elongation and nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA642098; cytokinin metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS3492
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ipt4-1 and ipt3-2 ipt4-1 had the lowest iP levels after inoculation in high nitrate; nlp1 or nlp4 mutations genetically rescue ipt4 nitrate hypersensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjIpt4 is a core cytokinin biosynthetic enzyme supporting iP accumulation in the root susceptible zone. 2. High nitrate lowers effective symbiotic cytokinin output and ipt4 mutants become hypersensitive in nodule initiation, maturation, and nitrogen fixation. 3. Nlp1/Nlp4 act upstream of LjIpt4-dependent cytokinin biosynthesis because nlp mutations rescue the ipt4 nitrate-sensitive phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: LjIpt4 is a candidate target to sustain nodulation under nitrate-rich soils, potentially in combination with modulation of nitrate signaling genes, while avoiding excessive cytokinin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nodule organogenesis, nodule maturation, and symbiotic nitrogen fixation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cytokinin biosynthesis and nodulation signaling under nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of symbiotic signaling and cytokinin biosynthesis in response to nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for nitrate-dependent repression of Ipt2, Log1, Log4, RR5, RR9, Nfr1, Nfr5, Nsp2, Ern1 and Nin
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ipt2; Log1; Log4; RR5; RR9; Nfr1; Nfr5; Nsp2; Ern1; Nin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major nitrate-responsive controller required to restrict symbiotic cytokinin accumulation and downstream nodulation under high nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Nlp1 abolishes nitrate inhibition of cytokinin biosynthesis/signaling and confers nitrate-resistant nodulation and nitrogenase activity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrate (5 mM KNO3 or 10 mM KNO3); rhizobial inoculation with Mesorhizobium loti; exogenous BA in spontaneous nodulation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LORE1 loss-of-function mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; cytokinin quantification; nodulation scoring; acetylene reduction assay; spontaneous nodule assay; genetic interaction with ipt4
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: nlp1 mutants form slightly fewer nodules in nitrate-free conditions, indicating contribution to general symbiotic gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA642098; cytokinin metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS3492
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes NLP proteins can cooperate with symbiotic regulation at NIN binding sites and may both activate and repress symbiotic genes depending on context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjNlp1 is required for nitrate-mediated repression of cytokinin biosynthesis genes and cytokinin-responsive RR genes in inoculated roots. 2. Loss of LjNlp1 prevents nitrate-induced reduction of root iP and sustains nodule maturation and nitrogen fixation under high nitrate. 3. LjNlp1 acts upstream of cytokinin biosynthesis by repressing NF signaling components and nodulation transcription factors under nitrate.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing LjNlp1 nitrate repression may improve biological nitrogen fixation in nitrate-containing soils, but complete loss may alter normal nitrate responses and slightly reduce nodulation in low nitrate.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nodule organogenesis, nodule maturation, and symbiotic nitrogen fixation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cytokinin biosynthesis and nodulation signaling under nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of symbiotic signaling and cytokinin biosynthesis in response to nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for nitrate-dependent repression of Ipt2, Log1, Log4, RR5, RR9, Nfr5, Nsp2, Ern1 and Nin; Nfr1 remains nitrate-repressed in nlp4-1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ipt2; Log1; Log4; RR5; RR9; Nfr5; Nsp2; Ern1; Nin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major nitrate-responsive controller required to restrict symbiotic cytokinin accumulation and nodulation under high nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Nlp4 abolishes nitrate inhibition of cytokinin biosynthesis/signaling and enhances mature nodule formation and nitrogenase activity in nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrate (5 mM KNO3 or 10 mM KNO3); rhizobial inoculation with Mesorhizobium loti; exogenous BA in spontaneous nodulation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LORE1 loss-of-function mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; cytokinin quantification; nodulation scoring; acetylene reduction assay; spontaneous nodule assay; genetic interaction with ipt4
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In spontaneous nodulation assay nlp4-1 retained nitrate sensitivity, indicating partial pathway specificity relative to nlp1
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA642098; cytokinin metabolomics, MetaboLights MTBLS3492
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously called NRSYM1; discussion notes LjNlp4 directly targets LjCLE-RS2 in AON, but this was background/context rather than the main tested metabolite-causality focus here
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjNlp4 is required for nitrate-mediated repression of symbiotic cytokinin biosynthesis genes and cytokinin signaling markers. 2. nlp4 mutants maintain cytokinin levels and show nitrate-resistant nodule maturation and partially resistant nitrogen fixation. 3. LjNlp4 acts upstream of cytokinin biosynthesis by repressing multiple NF signaling and nodulation genes under nitrate.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning LjNlp4 could help sustain nodulation in nitrate-containing soils, potentially alongside cytokinin biosynthetic targets, but pathway-specific effects on autoregulation and nitrate signaling should be considered.
